unit.name: wpdb public-state expanded storage adapter shell proof
unit.public_contract: WordPress-compatible wpdb remains a PHP-visible class/global object with declared public fields, dynamic properties, magic accessors, protected __set write-block behavior, field_types direct mutation, and db.php replacement timing intact while typed Haxe owns expanded public/magic state default and write-routing policy.

owns: all declared public property default kind/value policy
expanded magic-visible property default kind/value policy except lazy col_info
native PHP array default shape policy

preserved: global $wpdb can be supplied by wp-content/db.php
direct public property reads/writes still use PHP object fields
expanded magic-visible internals route through adapter-backed shell storage
protected magic write blocks remain compatible
field_types and table-list defaults remain native PHP arrays
dbh/result remain native-resource boundaries at the PHP shell
php_abi_shell.proof: WPHX-305.25 compares a Haxe-adapter-backed PHP shell against a constructor-free WordPress 7.0 wpdb oracle and also installs that shell through a temp db.php drop-in.

bridge.reason: This slice moves all declared public defaults plus expanded magic-visible state routing into typed Haxe while keeping mixed native PHP values in the PHP ABI shell. That avoids Dynamic and raw php.Syntax.code while proving public/magic behavior against WordPress before attempting complete wpdb class ownership.
bounded_by: WordPress 7.0 constructor-free wpdb public/magic behavior oracle
Haxe-adapter-backed PHP shell state snapshot comparison
WPHX-305.21 row materialization live candidate receipt

removal_gate.condition: Do not replace the full wpdb class or require_wp_db() bootstrap until actual mysqli resources, lazy col_info materialization, constructor side effects, live plugin reflection probes, and bootstrap loading are proven under broader WordPress probes.
